items.headline: Pakistan mediates two-week Iran-US ceasefire ending six-week Strait of Hormuz blockade
items.body: Iran agreed to reopen waterway carrying quarter of global oil supplies hours before threatened infrastructure strikes. Temporary halt pushes decisions on energy access and nuclear containment into diplomatic uncertainty.

items.headline: Myanmar junta leader formalizes civilian presidency through controlled parliament vote
items.body: Min Aung Hlaing won approval five years after coup that deposed elected government. Transition from military uniform consolidates authoritarian rule while civil war continues across territory junta cannot control.

items.headline: Southern Africa cholera cases surge sevenfold as cyclone flooding overwhelms water systems
items.body: Over four thousand cases and 56 deaths reported in six weeks across five countries. Mozambique accounts for ninety percent of infections as climate-amplified outbreaks reverse continental transmission declines.

items.headline: Cuba releases over two thousand prisoners as oil blockade pressures government
items.body: Pardon coincided with Holy Week but followed months of energy collapse after oil shipments halted. Release marks largest amnesty in decade amid intensifying confrontation over political detentions and economic survival.

meanwhile.search: US political polarization 1988 2026 left shift study

es.headline: Former transport minister faces Supreme Court trial over pandemic procurement kickbacks
es.body: A corruption trial opened against a close ally who helped propel current government to power. Parliamentary suspension could strip ruling coalition of crucial vote as multiple graft probes converge on minority administration.

uk.headline: Resident doctors begin six-day strike disrupting NHS services across England
uk.body: Junior doctors walked out demanding pay restoration after years of real-term cuts. Extended action forces hospitals to cancel appointments and procedures during longest continuous industrial action by doctors.

nl.headline: Dutch bank ING trapped in Russia as Kremlin blocks sale of operations
nl.body: Moscow refused regulatory approval for the sale to a Russian buyer after determining it needs Western banks for international payments. The blocked exit leaves ING facing ongoing exposure despite terminating all new Russian business since the Ukraine invasion.

nl.headline: Senate majority prepares to block government plan to accelerate pension age increases
nl.body: Cross-party opposition argues the proposal violates a 2019 agreement between government, employers and unions on retirement age indexation. Vote scheduled for Wednesday threatens minority cabinet's first major policy test.
